title: fix(gen-image): honor aspectRatio for grok-video when width/height omitted
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
- `params.ts` fills width/height with a square default (1024) when dims are omitted, so `xaiVideoModel` collapsed every request to 1:1 and ignored the explicit `aspectRatio` param.
- Now gate on `dimensionsExplicit`: derive from width/height only when the caller passed them; otherwise pass the explicit `aspectRatio` label through (validated against supported `ASPECT_RATIOS`).
- Adds `test/image/xaiVideoModel.test.ts` (explicit-aspectRatio, explicit-dims, square-default fallback) — 4 pass, existing wan tests unchanged.

Risks: low — behavior unchanged when width/height are provided; only the no-dims path changes, and unsupported labels (21:9/9:21/adaptive) fall back to the previous square-default ratio.

Fixes #9099

gist.headline: No more forced squares
gist.blurb: Grok video generation now properly respects your requested aspect ratio when you don't specify exact dimensions. No more accidental squares when you wanted widescreen!
gist.summary: The grok-video model now correctly honors the aspectRatio parameter when explicit width and height dimensions are omitted, rather than defaulting to a 1024x1024 square.
gist.impact: Users generating videos with Grok without specifying exact dimensions will now receive their requested aspect ratio instead of everything defaulting to 1:1.
gist.visual_concept: The bee mascot acting as a film director, stretching a square video frame into a widescreen rectangle. The monitor robot assisting with a clapperboard.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing a tiny director's beret, pulling and stretching a glowing lime green (#ecf874) video frame from a square into a wide rectangle. A chunky retro monitor robot watches happily, holding a clapperboard. Soft pastel lavender and cream gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Nostalgic but beautiful, emotionally warm.
